    bootstrapping

    bootstrapping.Rmd

    Bootstrapping of Embeded Standard Setting

    Overview

    How to use bootstrapping for ESS

    Fit Embeded Standard Setting 

    library(EmStanS)
    # data <- read.csv(filePath)
fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3, 
               cor_val = 0.2,
               n = 30,
               nlevel = 3,
               ntable = 5,
               npanelist = 5,
               sdinp = 1,
               ecinp = 0,
               100,300)



fake_data
#> $setup
#> # A tibble: 3 × 7
#>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options`
#>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>          
#> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2…
#> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2…
#> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2…
#> # ℹ 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl>
#> 
#> $panelist
#> # A tibble: 75 × 3
#>   GCA   Panelist Table
#>   <chr> <chr>    <chr>
#> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1   
#> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1   
#> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1   
#> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1   
#> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1   
#> # ℹ 70 more rows
#> 
#> $rating
#> # A tibble: 2,250 × 8
#>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD   
#>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr> 
#> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1
#> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1
#> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level1
#> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level2
#> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2
#> # ℹ 2,245 more rows
#> 
#> $item_data
#> # A tibble: 90 × 7
#>   GCA   Domain Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50
#>   <chr> <chr>  <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl>
#> 1 M1    Alg    M1_1               1     1                   1     102.
#> 2 M1    Prob   M1_2               1     1                   2     107.
#> 3 M1    Calc   M1_3               1     1                   3     110.
#> 4 M1    Geom   M1_4               1     1                   4     113.
#> 5 M1    Geom   M1_5               1     1                   5     116.
#> # ℹ 85 more rows
#> 
#> $examineedata_data
#> # A tibble: 664 × 3
#>   score  freq Grade
#>   <int> <int> <chr>
#> 1    86     8 M1   
#> 2    87    11 M1   
#> 3    88     4 M1   
#> 4    89     3 M1   
#> 5    90     3 M1   
#> # ℹ 659 more rows

# Or you can use your own data structured by the following table.
    res <- emstans(data = fake_data,
               grade = c(1,2),
               targets = "ALD",
               WESS = T,
               gamest = F,
               median = "modal",
               loc = "Loc_RP50",
               domain = "GCA")

    Bootstrapping

    • Only one GCA feasible
    boot_res <- bootstrapping(res, "M1-All", 10)

    Report 

    report(boot_res)
    • M1-All
    • Boot plot
    • Boot table
    • Boot summary 
    extract(boot_res)
#> $bootstrapping_cutscore
#>       L2_W    L3_W
#> 1  140.983 248.362
#> 2  115.349 255.997
#> 3  113.419 243.634
#> 4  158.901 262.915
#> 5  110.398 257.536
#> 6  127.626 276.032
#> 7  101.369 268.906
#> 8  162.340 256.060
#> 9  168.672 242.028
#> 10 127.023 253.572
#> 
#> $bootstrapping_summary
#>       n   mean    sd median    min    max range skew kurtosis   se
#> L2_W 10 132.61 23.89 127.32 101.37 168.67  67.3 0.28    -1.66 7.56
#> L3_W 10 256.50 10.69 256.03 242.03 276.03  34.0 0.32    -1.11 3.38
#> 
#> $bootstrapping_plot
